user_query:
I need a clean, auditable quantity and cost roll-up from our element export. Please do all processing with Pandas and produce four deliverables. Read these inputs: input/elements.csv, input/unit_prices.csv, and input/markup.json.

Requirements:
1) Data cleaning and filtering
- Normalize Category and Material strings by trimming whitespace and converting to Title Case.
- Drop rows where Exclude is true (case-insensitive truthy: true/1/yes) OR Phase equals "Demolition" (case-insensitive).
- Ensure Volume_m3 and Area_m2 are numeric (coerce invalids to 0.0), and Level is a string.

2) Pricing merge and calculations
- Merge prices on both Category and Material from input/unit_prices.csv (columns: Category, Material, Unit_Price).
- For any element with no matching price, set Unit_Price to 0 and add Missing_Price=True; otherwise Missing_Price=False.
- Load category-specific markup rates from input/markup.json (keys are Category; values are decimals, e.g., 0.12 for 12%). If a category is missing from the JSON, apply a default markup rate of 0.10.
- Compute Base_Cost = Volume_m3 * Unit_Price and Total_Cost = Base_Cost * (1 + Markup_Rate).

3) Files to write under output/
- output/detailed_elements.csv: columns exactly in this order: ElementId, Category, Material, Level, Volume_m3, Area_m2, Unit_Price, Markup_Rate, Base_Cost, Total_Cost, Missing_Price. Sort rows by Level ascending, then Category ascending, then ElementId ascending. Numeric values may be full precision (no forced rounding needed here).
- output/summary_by_category_material.csv: group by Category and Material, with columns: Category, Material, element_count, total_volume, total_area, avg_volume, base_cost, total_cost, volume_pct. Where:
  • element_count = number of elements in group
  • total_volume = sum Volume_m3
  • total_area = sum Area_m2
  • avg_volume = mean Volume_m3
  • base_cost = sum Base_Cost
  • total_cost = sum Total_Cost
  • volume_pct = total_volume / (sum of Volume_m3 across all kept elements) * 100
  Round all numeric columns in this summary to 2 decimals. Sort by total_volume descending.
- output/pivot_by_level_category.csv: a pivot of Total_Cost sums with Level as rows and Category as columns. Fill missing with 0. Include an extra column named Total that is the row-wise sum across category columns.
- output/report.json: a JSON object with keys: total_base_cost, total_cost, total_volume, missing_price_count, levels, categories. Where totals are sums over the filtered dataset (not rounded beyond 2 decimals); missing_price_count is the count of Missing_Price==True in detailed_elements; levels is a sorted unique list of all Level values present; categories is a sorted unique list of all Category values present.

Notes:
- Only read from input/ and only write to output/ as specified.
- Do not include any other columns in the outputs beyond what is requested.
- Ensure all files are written even if some groups/pivot cells have zeros.
